Title: MoE to build eight new special education schools
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Thirty-eight special education school projects approved under the 2026 Budget are currently in the planning phase, the Dewan Rakyat was told today.
Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the ministry had also received approval for the construction of eight new Special Education Schools, comprising one project under the 11th Malaysia Plan (11MP), five under 12MP and two under 13MP.
"Meanwhile, 80 projects involving the construction of new or additional buildings for (the Special Education Integration Programme) have been approved during the 12MP period.
"Of these, five projects have been completed, 22 are under construction and 53 are in the pre-construction phase," he said.
He was responding to Tan Kok Wai (PH-Cheras) on the total number and categories of children registered with learning disabilities in the country, as well as the current state of the special education system in meeting their educational needs.
Wong said the ministry was also utilising the Industrialised Building System modular construction system to accelerate the delivery of special education infrastructure.
Through this approach, he said, 40 Special Education Integrated Programme classrooms in 10 schools built in 2025 have begun operations, while another 241 classrooms in 46 schools are expected to be completed this year and become operational in 2027.
He said the expansion of facilities was necessary as the number of special educational needs continued to increase.
As of May 31 this year, said Wong, 161,891 special educational needs students were registered under the ministry.
He said, of this number, 141,545 students, or about 87 per cent, were categorised as having learning disabilities.
"This includes 53,169 students with autism, 39,254 with intellectual disabilities, 24,148 with specific learning difficulties, 13,337 with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, 4,501 with Down syndrome, 3,741 with global developmental delay and 3,395 under other categories," he said.
Wong said 7,406 schools nationwide were implementing special education programmes as of May 31, comprising 35 special education schools, 2,907 schools offering the Special Education Integrated Programme and 4,406 schools implementing the Inclusive Education Programme.
He said the ministry, through the Malaysia Education Plan 2026-2035, would continue focusing on improving physical infrastructure and the well-being of students with special educational needs to accommodate increasing enrolment.
On meeting the demand for special education teachers, Wong said 95.8 per cent, or 21,314 out of 22,254 positions nationwide, had been filled as of July 9.
He said 1,055 new special education teachers had been appointed between 2023 and July this year to fill Education Service Officer Grade DG9 vacancies nationwide.
Of this number, 664 were placed in primary schools, while 391 were placed in secondary schools.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Halal Home Grown Champion programme generates RM187.91 million potential sales
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Halal Home Grown Champion — Sourcing Partnership 2.0 programme has assisted 313 halal companies, generating RM187.91 million in potential sales between 2024 and this year.
The Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry said the beneficiaries included 158 Bumiputera-owned firms and 52 women-owned businesses.
The ministry described the initiative as a targeted intervention programme designed to help local micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) scale up.
In a written reply posted on the Parliament website today, the ministry also noted that the government, through the Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation (Matrade), will organise the Malaysia International Halal Showcase (Mihas) 2026.
The world's largest halal trade show is scheduled to run from Sept 23 to 26 at the Malaysia International Trade and Exhibition Centre (Mitec) in Kuala Lumpur.
"A total of 2,400 exhibition booths will be provided, and more than 1,000 local MSMEs are expected to benefit from the business opportunities created by the event," it said.
The ministry was responding to a question from Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin (PN-Pagoh) regarding specific strategies for 2026 to penetrate new markets, support local halal MSMEs, increase product value-added, and reduce dependence on traditional markets.
The ministry said Malaysia has a strong foundation to maintain its position as a global leader in the halal industry, adding that the country is well-positioned to expand its share of the global halal market and boost the sector's contribution to the national economy, driven by its globally trusted halal certification reputation, a comprehensive halal ecosystem, and targeted market exploration strategies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MACC to station integrity officer at Perkeso following Daya Kerjaya 2.0 fraud probe
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) will immediately station a Certified Integrity Officer (CeIO) at the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso).
The move follows governance weaknesses uncovered during investigations into the Daya Kerjaya 2.0 incentive fraud case.
MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said Perkeso had requested an integrity officer to be placed at the agency after the case came to light.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: THMY builds Penang factory to meet AI, data centre demand
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: THMY Holdings Bhd has broken ground on a new factory and corporate headquarters at the Batu Kawan Industrial Park in Penang as it expands capacity to meet rising demand from the artificial intelligence (AI), data centre and cloud computing sectors.
The new facility, targeted for completion by the second quarter of 2028, will underpin the company's next phase of growth by strengthening its manufacturing, engineering and operational capabilities.
Purpose-built to support higher production volumes, the factory will initially house 20 advanced automated test solution workstations, enabling THMY to meet increasingly sophisticated customer testing requirements.
THMY, an automated test solutions provider for the electrical and electronics (E&E) industry, said the investment will strengthen the company's ability to capitalise on the growing demand for automated test solutions, particularly across data centre, artificial intelligence and cloud computing applications, while reinforcing its position within the regional E&E manufacturing ecosystem.
It added that the factory will provide additional production floor space to the company, significantly increasing its manufacturing capacities and engineering capabilities to scale its operations in order to undertake larger and more complex projects.
Executive director/chief executive officer Ooi Can Nix said the groundbreaking ceremony marks a significant milestone in the company's growth journey.
"This new factory and corporate office reflect our confidence in the long-term prospects of the automated test solutions industry and our commitment towards strengthening our manufacturing capabilities to support customers' evolving requirements.
"The new factory will significantly enhance our production capacity and engineering capabilities. This investment positions THMY to capture growing opportunities while laying a strong foundation for our next phase of sustainable growth," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Artificial cloud brightening could tame El Nino, but with risks
Article:
A brewing "Super" El Nino cycle is poised to unleash heat waves, floods and drought worldwide, with the effects amplified by long term human-caused climate change.
But what if it was possible to interrupt and effectively "switch off" the Pacific Ocean heating phenomenon as it starts to form?
That's the premise of a new Science Advances study published Wednesday, which uses computer models to show that artificially brightening clouds over the Pacific, when timed right, could neutralise the influential weather pattern and blunt its worst impacts.
While previous research on so-called geoengineering has focused on cooling the planet as a whole, the new paper instead proposes more targeted interventions.
"These shorter timescales of interventions could be a very powerful way that geoengineering enters this portfolio of responses to climate change," lead author Jessica Wan, a postdoctoral researcher at the University of Chicago, told AFP.
Prior research had shown that the "Black Summer" bushfires that scorched Australia in 2019-2020 played a key role in creating a multi-year La Nina, the cool phase of the El Nino-Southern Oscillation cycle.
As smoke particles entered the clouds, they set off chain reactions that actually made the clouds brighter, bouncing more of the Sun's energy back to space.
Motivated by this "natural experiment," Wan and her colleagues wondered whether solar geoengineering could achieve a similar effect.
They used a powerful forecasting model to gauge what impact artificial marine cloud brightening over a vast rectangular zone in the equatorial Pacific would have had on the 1997-1998 and 2015-16 El Nino events.
This could theoretically be achieved by using ships fitted with nozzles that spray sea salt into the lower atmosphere.
El Ninos are linked with warming sea surfaces in the eastern tropical Pacific, with knock on effects globally including drier conditions and drought in Australia, wetter winters in East Africa, and warmer overall global temperatures.
After running several simulations, they found the intervention worked best when started early – in June – and continued through the following February.
"But the reason people would ever care about this is not temperature in a box in the Pacific, but how the impacts translate over land," said Wan.
Extreme weather events linked to El Nino have caused loss of life and trillions of dollars of economic damage.
The modeled cloud brightening reversed most of the temperature and precipitation effects – those areas that got warmer under an El Nino were cooler, regions that got wetter instead became drier, and vice versa.
So what's the catch? For starters, the technology isn't yet ready.
Teams around the world are developing the nozzles to spray sea salt into the sky, but the devices can't yet shoot nearly enough volume. Even if a nozzle is perfected, it would take roughly 2,400 ships to make the required impact.
Beyond the engineering challenges, there are further risks.
Not all regions of the world would benefit from reversing El Nino conditions, and the models showed unintended consequences, including warming over Europe and Asia.
"The other catch I would say is that we didn't look at long-term impacts," said Wan, including what would happen if marine cloud brightening was used repeatedly to suppress El Ninos.
Geoengineering has many detractors, who argue it creates a moral hazard by giving polluters a free pass to keep emitting if the climate can be artificially cooled. Wan disagrees.
"We're beyond the point now where we could just turn off emissions today and be totally fine, we're locked into warming already, and so the way that I viewed geoengineering is 'how can we reduce the worst of those impacts while we work on a long-term solution?'" she said.
"I think it would be irresponsible to not do the research, given that's the context we're in."
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] MACC freezes RM3.4mil in company accounts in RM20mil false documents probe
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has frozen company accounts containing RM3.4 million as part of an investigation into the alleged submission of false documents to obtain about RM20 million in financing from a development financial institution.
MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said the funds were frozen in the course of the investigation.
He added that two company directors and an operations manager had been detained to assist in the probe.
"We have also recorded statements from 10 individuals and will summon another 23 witnesses.
"Initial investigations found that information relating to the purchase of padi and rice used to obtain the financing was false.
"We will obtain additional information to complete the investigation before taking further action," he told reporters at the MACC headquarters today.
The case involves a company engaged in the production and processing of padi and rice, whose representatives are alleged to have submitted documents containing false particulars in connection with Trade Working Capital Financing-I facilities.
It was previously reported that the MACC arrested three individuals, including two company directors, over the alleged submission of the documents.
The suspects are believed to have sought financing of about RM20 million despite no actual transaction involving the purchase of padi and rice having taken place.
The operations manager, a man in his 30s, was remanded for five days until July 11 after Magistrate Eliana Ismail issued the order at the Alor Setar Civil Court on Tuesday.
He was detained on Monday evening after turning up to give his statement at the MACC office in Alor Setar.
Investigators believe the three suspects, who are family members, conspired to submit the false documents in 2025.
The two other suspects, both company directors in their 60s, were later released on MACC bail after their statements were recorded.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Australia, India strike deal on uranium exports during Modi visit
Article:
NEW DELHI: Australia and India reached a deal on Thursday to export Australian uranium to India for use in the nuclear energy industry, while agreeing to deepen cooperation in renewables, critical minerals and green hydrogen.
India has long eyed Australia's uranium reserves to help meet a target of 100 gigawatts of nuclear energy capacity by 2047, while Australia is looking to diversify trade beyond its reliance on China, its top partner.
"Australia and India are close partners and even closer friends," Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese told reporters in Melbourne on Thursday, after finalising the deal with visiting Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi.
"The arrangement facilitates Australian uranium exports to India to help increase the share of non-fossil fuel power capacity, providing an additional market for the Australian resources sector."
Though both nations agreed to a nuclear cooperation pact in 2014, uranium exports have been limited over concerns about ensuring nuclear fuel is used solely for peaceful purposes, such as energy generation.
Modi said on Thursday India's relationship with Australia presented "historic opportunities" for both countries to cooperate across several areas.
Australia's technology, capital and resources could help accelerate India's energy transition, Modi said. He also signalled possible cooperation in low-carbon aluminium projects.
"We have historic opportunities to cooperate in this field," Modi said, as he urged Australia's business community to invest long-term in India's road, port, rail and urban infrastructure projects.
"India provides a safe, stable and sustainable growth option for your funds," he said. Australia's largest pension fund, AustralianSuper, said on Thursday it would invest a further A$500 million (US$347 million) in India's National Investment and Infrastructure Fund.
After meeting Modi at the business event, Albanese called the Indian leader a "living bridge" between Australia and India, saying Modi's vision had helped reshape the roadmap for Australia's economic engagement with India.
India is Australia's fifth-largest trading partner after China, Japan, the US and South Korea, while around one million people in Australia claim Indian ancestry, out of a population of 28 million.
Modi, who previously visited Australia in 2023, is expected to meet thousands of expatriate Indians at an event in one of the biggest stadiums in Melbourne on Thursday evening.
The Indian leader has staged large-scale events during his overseas trips and has addressed packed stadiums in Britain, the United States and other countries that have large expatriate Indian populations.
Thousands of supporters thronged one of Sydney's biggest indoor stadiums during his last visit three years ago.
Modi arrived in Australia after visiting Indonesia, where he signed a raft of deals on agriculture and defence, including for the BrahMos cruise missile system.
He will leave for New Zealand on Friday afternoon before returning to India.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court stays Bar's challenge to Zahid's DNAA pending Federal Court appeal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court adjourned the Malaysian Bar's judicial review hearing challenging the discharge not amounting to an acquittal (DNAA) granted to Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi pending his Federal Court appeal in October.
Judge Alice Loke granted the adjournment following a joint application by senior federal counsel Ahmad Hanir Hambaly @ Arwi and Datuk Ambiga Sreenevasan, who leads the Bar's legal team.
Loke fixed Oct 12 for case management to update the court on the Federal Court proceedings.
Earlier, Ahmad Hanir sought the adjournment because Zahid's leave application, scheduled for hearing on Oct 8, would determine the legal test governing judicial review in matters involving prosecutorial discretion. Ambiga did not object to the adjournment application.
The Malaysian Bar filed the judicial review in 2023 to challenge the Attorney General's Chambers' decision to discontinue all 47 corruption, criminal breach of trust, and money laundering charges against Zahid in the Yayasan Akalbudi case. Zahid is named as the second respondent.
In May, a three-member Court of Appeal bench led by Datuk Faizah Jamaludin reinstated the Bar's judicial review application and remitted the matter to the High Court for a substantive hearing on the merits.
The appellate court decision overturned a June 27, 2024 ruling by High Court judge Datuk Amarjeet Singh, who has since been elevated to the Court of Appeal.
https://www.nst.com.my/amp/news/nation/2026/05/1434037/updated-court-appeal-allows-malaysian-bar-challenge-zahids-dnaa
Amarjeet had dismissed the Bar's application for leave to initiate the judicial review, ruling that the Bar failed to rebut the legal presumption that the attorney-general acted lawfully.
He also held that the attorney-general was not obliged to disclose the reasons for discontinuing the prosecution.
Zahid was granted a discharge not amounting to a DNAA on Sept 4, 2023, after the prosecution applied to discontinue the case.
Federal Court judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah, who presided as the High Court judge, refused to grant a full acquittal, noting the attorney-general's authority to withdraw charges before judgment.
Sequerah also said that judicial time and a large amount of taxpayers' money would have been wasted if the prosecution decided not to proceed after the High Court had already found a prima facie case on all 47 charges and ordered Zahid to enter his defence.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Singapore police arrest man over bomb threats targeting Parliament, HTX
Article:
SINGAPORE: A 40-year-old man was arrested for his suspected involvement in two anonymous bomb threats targeting Parliament House and the Home Team Science and Technology Agency (HTX) office, Singapore police said.
In a statement, police said the man will be charged in court on Thursday with communicating false information about a harmful thing under Section 268A of the Penal Code 1871.
The police said HTX received an online form submission on July 3 stating that a bomb had been placed in its building.
Separately, police received a report that an anonymous email had been sent to the Prime Minister's Office stating that a bomb had been placed at Parliament House on July 7.
Police conducted thorough checks of both premises, including systematic sweeps of the buildings and their surroundings, and found no items of security concern.
Officers from the Criminal Investigation Department and Central Police Division established that the two bomb threats were sent by the same person and arrested the man on July 7, 2026.
Several electronic devices believed to have been used to perpetrate the threats were seized from him as case exhibits, police said in a statement on Wednesday.
– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Married couple among five nabbed in RM13.3mil ganja buds bust
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A married couple believed to be linked to a ganja buds trafficking syndicate were among five detained after police seized more than RM13 million worth of drugs in raids at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) and Kota Warisan, Sepang.
Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said police seized 166.2kg of ganja buds worth RM13.34 million during the raids.
"The success was achieved through four raids conducted by the KLIA district police headquarters Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID) with the cooperation of Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) KLIA auxiliary police," he said at a press conference at the police headquarters here.
Shazeli said in the first case on June 29, KLIA auxiliary police detected two suspicious bags while they were being screened at the KLIA baggage scanning machine before arresting a 25-year-old foreign man at the flight departure waiting area.
"Checks on both bags found 60 plastic packets containing compressed lumps believed to be ganja buds weighing about 33.6kg," he said in a statement on Wednesday.
Shazeli said in the second raid on the same day, police again detected a suspicious bag at the KLIA terminal before arresting a 35-year-old local woman believed to be the owner of the bag.
"Checks found 39 plastic packets containing compressed lumps believed to be ganja buds weighing 21.1kg, with the seized drugs estimated to be worth RM1.7 million," he said.
He said on July 3, police arrested a married couple, aged 31 and 40 respectively, at a condominium car park in Kota Warisan after they were suspected of being involved in drug distribution activities.
"Following interrogation, we raided a condominium unit rented by the couple and found four bags and three plastic bags containing 146 plastic packets filled with compressed lumps believed to be ganja buds.
"The seizure was estimated to weigh 76kg, with a total value of RM6.1 million," he said.
Shazeli said the couple were suspected of being actively involved in drug trafficking activities for the past two years.
"We also seized a Perodua Alza vehicle worth RM20,000, which was believed to have been used for drug delivery activities," he said.
In the final raid on July 4, Shazeli said MAHB KLIA auxiliary police detected two more suspicious bags at the KLIA terminal before arresting a 27-year-old foreign man believed to be their owner.
"Further inspection found 68 plastic packets containing compressed lumps believed to be ganja buds weighing 35.5kg, with the seizure valued at RM2.84 million," he said.s
Shazeli said further investigations found that three local suspects had previous records involving drug-related and criminal offences.s
"Initial urine screening found that only two local suspects tested positive for drugs. All suspects have been remanded to assist investigations under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act for drug trafficking," he said.s
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia, Thailand to resolve fisheries market access issue within a week
Article:
PUTRAJAYA:  Malaysia and Thailand have agreed to resolve outstanding fisheries market access issues by expediting the implementation of a related memorandum of understanding (MoU) within a week.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said both countries' ministers had committed to fast-tracking the agreement to ensure a swift resolution.
"We have resolved the outstanding issues related to fisheries. Both ministers initially proposed implementing the memorandum within one-and-a-half months.
"But we agreed for it to be implemented within a week," he said at a joint press conference with Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul, who is on a two-day official visit to Malaysia.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Negara keeps OPR at 2.75pct as growth, inflation stay balanced
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia has maintained the overnight policy rate (OPR) at 2.75 per cent, extending its pause in monetary policy that has been in place since July 2025.
The central bank said the current monetary policy stance remains appropriate to support sustainable economic growth while preserving price stability.
In a statement following its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, Bank Negara said Malaysia's economy remained resilient in the second quarter.
This is supported by sustained domestic demand and stronger-than-expected exports despite ongoing geopolitical uncertainties.
Bank Negara said growth would continue to be underpinned by strong labour market conditions, wage growth and policy measures that support household spending.
"The MPC will remain vigilant to ongoing developments and assess the balance of risks surrounding the outlook for domestic inflation and growth," it said in a statement.
The central bank also expects the external sector to strengthen further, supported by improving global demand, particularly for electrical and electronics (E&E) products, a recovery in non-E&E exports and sustained tourism spending.
"Therefore, the growth projection for 2026 is expected to be firmly within the forecast range of 4.0−5.0 per cent.
"This growth outlook remains subject to downside risks from a prolonged conflict in the Middle East and lower commodity production," it said.
It noted that upside potential to growth could arise from a better global growth outlook following de-escalation of the conflict, stronger demand for E&E goods and higher tourism activity.
Globally, Bank Negara said growth continues to be supported by the expansion of the technology sector, improving supply conditions and easing prices of key commodities.
A sustained de-escalation of the Middle East conflict would further improve global supply chains.
Nevertheless, downside risks persist from geopolitical uncertainty, tighter global financial conditions and elevated valuations in financial markets.
On inflation, headline inflation averaged 1.7 per cent while core inflation stood at 2.1 per cent in the first five months of 2026, broadly in line with expectations despite some initial pass-through from higher global costs.
While elevated global commodity prices resulting from developments in the Middle East are expected to exert upward pressure on inflation, Bank Negara said the overall impact on both headline and core inflation this year should remain contained, supported by domestic policy measures and stable demand conditions.
"The MPC considers the current monetary policy stance to be appropriate and consistent with the outlook of continued price stability and sustainable economic growth.
"The MPC will remain vigilant to ongoing developments and assess the balance of risks surrounding the outlook for domestic inflation and growth," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's IPI rose 8.4pct in May, says DOSM
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The industrial production index (IPI) rose 8.4 per cent year-on-year (YoY)  in May driven by strong growth across manufacturing, mining and electricity sectors.
The Statistics Department said that the manufacturing sector rose 6.6 per cent in May, coupled with a 19.8 per cent climb in the production of the mining sector.
The electricity sector grew 4.2 per cent.
"The robust growth of 19.8 per cent in the mining sector in May was due to the accelerated in natural gas output by 37.4 per cent.
"Furthermore, the crude oil and condensate production recorded a decrease of 0.7 per cent," it said.
As compared to the previous month, the mining index decreased by 2.2 per cent after registering a decline of 1.6 per cent in the preceding month.
It added that the generation of electricity grew  4.2 per cent YoY in May 2026. On a month-on-month basis, the electricity index grew by 1.3 per cent.
In comparison to the previous month, the IPI posted a 1.3 per cent turnaround against a decline of 3.4 per cent recorded in April 2026.
"The production of manufacturing sector in May was primarily spurred by growth in export-oriented industries at 8.8 per cent, compared to 8.5 per cent registered in the previous month.
"This increase was predominantly observed in the manufacture of computer, electronics and optical products which remained double-digit growth by 17 per cent," said the department in a statement.
In addition, the manufacture of coke and refined petroleum products also increased by 7.8 per cent.
The department added that the expansion of export-oriented industries mirrored the growth in the country's export of manufactured goods during this month.
On a month-on-month comparison, the export-oriented industries recorded a turnaround by 5.7 per cent from a decline of  8.7 per cent in April 2026.
Additionally, the domestic-oriented industries grew modestly by 2.0 per cent in May as opposed to the increase of 8.0 per cent registered in April.
"The sustained growth was particularly induced by the stable momentum recorded in manufacture of fabricated metal products, except machinery and equipment; and the manufacture of basic metals which registered an increases of 4.5 per cent and 7.3 per cent, respectively," said the department.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Energy Commission strengthens strategic ties with Media Prima
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Energy Commission (ST) conducted a corporate visit to Media Prima Bhd today to strengthen strategic ties and boost public awareness regarding energy sector utilisation.
The visit also served as a platform to share updates on the upcoming Karnival Bijak Tenaga 2026 (KBT2026), scheduled to take place from July 25 to 26 at Stadium Indera Mulia in Ipoh, Perak.
The ST delegation was led by Strategic Communications Section chief Veliana Ruslan, alongside her senior management team.
They arrived at 11.30am at the News and Current Affairs (NCA) editorial department, where they were received by Berita Harian (BH) associate editor Ahmad Naim Tahir.
Veliana said the collaboration with the media aimed to bring energy literacy narratives to a wider public audience.
"We want to educate the public to avoid excessive energy consumption and to use it more wisely through initiatives like the Karnival Bijak Tenaga and the Energy Literacy Ambassador Programme.
"ST will also make it easier for the public to deal with services, complaints, and energy issues, besides raising awareness on government policies and initiatives in the energy field, including the National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR)," she said.
Also present were Harian Metro group editor Husain Jahit, New Straits Times deputy group editor Sharanjit Singh, BH senior news editor Datuk Sarihah Mohd Ali, and Media Prima Omnia sales general manager (government) Shanaz Md Azali.
During the visit, the ST delegation was shown around the NCA editorial floor to observe the news publication process.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Ground-up governance drives Johor's economic growth, says Jafni
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor's economic growth was driven by a ground-up governance approach that prioritised the needs of the people and development, said caretaker state Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Md Jafni Md Shukor.
He said the model had helped strengthen investor confidence and drive economic growth in the state.
Jafni also credited Johor caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's 3T1M governance model for transforming Johor into Malaysia's fastest-growing state economy and attracting record investments.
He said the administration's emphasis on 'turun padang' (going to the ground), teamwork, think beyond and micromanage had enabled the state to respond quickly to issues, reduce bureaucracy and deliver stronger economic outcomes.
He said the approach differed from conventional governance as it required executive councillors and government agencies to personally inspect problems, coordinate solutions across departments, think beyond standard procedures and closely monitor implementation.
"When issues arise, whether floods, damaged roads or pollution, we go to the ground ourselves.
"It is not enough to hold meetings. We identify immediate, medium and long-term solutions, secure the necessary funding and ensure every agency plays its part," he said in an interview with the New Straits Times yesterday.
Jafni said the collaborative approach had helped increase Johor's annual state revenue from about RM1.6 billion when the administration took office in 2022 to a record RM2.67 billion in 2025.
He also highlighted Johor's economic performance, noting that the state's gross domestic product (GDP) expanded 6.4 per cent in 2024 before accelerating to 8.0 per cent in 2025, making it the fastest-growing state economy in the country.
On investment, Jafni attributed Johor's record RM110 billion in approved investments last year to reforms aimed at shortening approval timelines and improving investor confidence.
He said the state introduced a Fast Lane approval mechanism that allowed developers to begin earthworks within about two months for projects in areas already designated for industrial or commercial development, instead of waiting up to three years under previous procedures.
"Previously, development approvals could take between 24 and 36 months.
"Today, investors are seeing a government that is prepared to facilitate investment instead of delaying it."
Jafni said the streamlined process had strengthened Johor's competitiveness at a time when regional economies were competing for foreign investment.
He said Kulai had emerged as one of the biggest beneficiaries of the reforms, attracting nearly half of the state's investment inflows due to its extensive industrial land bank and established economic ecosystem.
"Kulai is growing rapidly as an industrial hub, with almost 6,000 hectares of planned industrial areas, including Senai Airport City, Indahpura and several major industrial parks.
"Investors choose Johor because they have confidence in the way we manage investments and expedite approvals."
Jafni said the state's economic achievements demonstrated that effective governance was measured not only by policy announcements but also by the ability to deliver tangible outcomes for businesses and the people.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid tells unity allies to move on from Najib-Rosmah issues in Johor campaign
Article:
KLUANG: Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi appeared to rebuke a fellow unity government component over the use of Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor's image in Johor election campaigning.
The deputy prime minister said politicians should stop revisiting issues involving Rosmah and former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak, and instead focus on matters concerning Johor.
He said issues involving Najib and Rosmah had already been extensively debated during the 15th General Election (GE15) and should no longer dominate political campaigning.
"I think our friends have run out of political capital. That's enough.
"Those issues were for GE15. They are over.
"This is Johor. Let's focus on Johor," he told reporters after attending the Engagement Session with the Johor Village Development and Security Committees (JPKK) here today.
Zahid was responding to questions over Rosmah's criticism of a recent campaign poster shared by DAP's Nga Kor Ming, which featured her image alongside the words: "Warning for all out-of-state voters in the Johor election. If you don't come back, I will be back."
Earlier today,
Rosmah lashed out at Nga
, describing the poster as "cheap election propaganda" and accusing the Housing and Local Government Minister of spreading false and misleading content.
In a Facebook post, she denied ever making the statement attributed to her in the poster, calling it "a clear lie" that was deliberately created to mislead voters.
Rosmah also urged Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil to take action against Nga, arguing that politicians should not be exempt from accountability for allegedly spreading false content online.
The poster, uploaded on Nga's X account in relation to Saturday's Johor election, has garnered more than 54,000 views.
On another matter, Zahid welcomed Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang's offer for
Pas to support Umno
in forming the Johor government should BN fail to secure a simple majority in Saturday's election.
"That's good. That's good. God willing," he said briefly.
Last night, Hadi said the Islamist party was prepared to help Umno form the next Johor state government if BN fell short of a simple majority.
He said Perikatan Nasional (PN) would also support Umno's candidate for the Menteri Besar post, while Pas would neither seek the top state position nor demand any state executive council appointments.
Hadi added that what mattered most to Pas was safeguarding "Malay-Muslim political power" while working alongside non-Muslims who were "not extreme."
Meanwhile, Zahid gave his assurance that BN would honour every pledge contained in its Johor election manifesto if it was returned to power.
"God willing, every pledge outlined in BN's manifesto will be fully implemented over the next term.
"At the national level, together with my colleagues in the leadership, I will continue to monitor the implementation and progress of the commitments contained in the manifesto."
Polling for the Johor election is scheduled for July 11, with early voting on July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PJ MP calls for comprehensive audit on outsourced IT contracts, including FWCMS
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A government backbencher has called for a comprehensive audit of all Information Technology (IT) public systems, and IT contracts outsourced to private companies, including the Foreign Worker Centralised Management System (FWCMS).
Petaling Jaya Member of Parliament Lee Chean Chung urged the government to review these platforms after claiming that the estimated annual revenue collected through FWCMS was 21-fold compared to the Prime Minister's Department's ICT service and supply contracts.
On an annualised basis, FWCMS fee collections are estimated to reach approximately RM458 million a year, or about RM1.3 million a day.
"The estimated annual revenue collected through FWCMS is about 20.87 times greater than the total value of active ICT services and supply contracts under the General Administration Division of the Prime Minister's Department.
"To put this into perspective, the National Unity Ministry was allocated RM767,993,300 under the 2026 Budget.
"This means the estimated annual FWCMS revenue of RM458 million is equivalent to nearly 60 per cent of the total allocation for an entire ministry," he said at a press conference at the parliament's media centre today.
Lee said that the proposed audit must also scrutinise the cybersecurity integrity of the government's IT systems and the personal data security of Malaysians.
"The audit should transparently assess three key areas: first, the level of cybersecurity and personal data protection.
"Second, whether the costs charged genuinely represent value for money.
"Third, whether the outsourcing model ultimately places excessive financial burden on employers, foreign workers, the public and taxpayers," he said.
Lee warned that the government must not allow public systems handling sensitive data, immigration processes, and the recruitment costs of foreign workers to operate without rigorous oversight.
"When a privately operated system can generate hundreds of millions of ringgit in annual revenue, the public has the right to know whether the fee structure is reasonable, transparent and truly serves the public interest," he said.
The comparisons drawn by Lee were based on official parliamentary records.
On June 25, 2026, Putrajaya in an oral reply said the total value of active ICT contracts under the General Administration Division of the Prime Minister's Department awarded to private companies between March 2025 and May 2026 amounted to RM21,922,645.51.
In contrast, a separate parliamentary reply dated Feb 11, 2026, stated that the private company operating the FWCMS collected RM381,293,065.80 in revenue over an 11-month period between Feb and Dec 2025.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: TFP Solutions unit lands Lee Chong Wei event deal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: TFP Solutions Bhd's unit Softfac Technology Sdn Bhd has secured a deal to provide exclusive payment collection and commercial infrastructure services for a live talk featuring Malaysian badminton legend Datuk Seri Lee Chong Wei.
TFP said Softfac had entered into a contract agreement with Ploutos, appointing it as the exclusive payment collection agent and commercial infrastructure provider for the "Beyond Greatness: From Inspiration to Impact" live talk.
The event is scheduled to take place on Oct 24-25 at Ideal Live Arena, Petaling Jaya.
The agreement also covers future live concerts, festivals, shows and corporate events organised by Ploutos during the one-year agreement period.
Softfac will provide front-end and back-end commercial infrastructure, real-time sales reporting modules and database support for ticket allocation through appointed ticketing agent Fantopia Sdn Bhd.
Ploutos, meanwhile, will oversee venue logistics, artiste bookings, event marketing, regulatory licensing and approvals.
"By securing exclusive rights to provide payment collection and ticketing platform services for Ploutos's events, the group establishes a recurring revenue model driven by platform usage, transaction processing, and future event deployments," TFP Solutions said in a bourse filing.
It said the agreement demonstrates the commercial viability of Softfac's proprietary technology, strengthening its ability to expand its digital solutions across the growing live entertainment, event management and digital commerce sectors.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chow: Penang not consulted on Kedah Sultan's call to review lease payment
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang was never consulted on the Sultan of Kedah's call for the federal government to review the state's lease payment, says Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow.
Chow said the matter falls under federal jurisdiction as the payment is made by Putrajaya and does not involve the state government.
"There has never been any discussion because the payment is made or contributed by the federal government. It does not involve the state," he told reporters today.
On July 5, the Sultan of Kedah, Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, called on the federal government
to review the lease payment
based on current rates, saying the existing payment no longer reflected the spirit and original intent of the agreement.
The sultan also reminded all parties to respect Kedah's rights and sovereignty as enshrined in the Federal Constitution.
Asked whether Penang supported the call, Chow declined to comment.
"I believe that question should be answered by the federal government," he said after his maiden visit to the Northern Heart Hospital.
Chow added that the Penang government had never received any legal correspondence from Kedah regarding the issue.
"Never," he said when asked whether the state had received letters from lawyers representing Kedah.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Labour leadership contest takes Burnham closer to UK PM's office
Article:
LONDON: Veteran politician Andy Burnham will take another step towards becoming the UK's next prime minister when nominations to replace Keir Starmer as Labour leader formally open on Thursday.
The 56-year-old is the only Labour member of parliament to have publicly said they are a candidate to succeed Starmer, who announced he was quitting last month.
Burnham will need to show that he has the support of 81 of Labour's 402 MPs to become a formal candidate, a tally that he is expected to surpass easily.
All eyes will be on whether an alternative candidate can muster the required support to challenge Burnham, nicknamed the "King of the North" for winning three consecutive Greater Manchester mayoral elections.
If no one sparks a contest, then Burnham will be crowned Labour's new leader – and prime minister in waiting – at a special conference on Friday 17 July.
Burnham would then replace Starmer in 10 Downing Street on Monday 20 July.
If a contest does take place, then the victor will be announced on 29 August following a ballot of Labour members and affiliated unions.
Burnham vowed in a keynote speech in late June to "bring about the biggest rebalancing of power our country has seen."
He pledged to put power "in the hands of the people and places who can use it best."
"We need a new determination to raise living standards of every single person in this land," he added at the speech in a Manchester museum.
"And we must accept that to do that, to fix the economy and the country, we need to change politics and we need to do it now."
[[nid:1484420]]
Starmer, under pressure for months over policy U-turns and questions about his judgement, announced on June 22 that he was resigning after losing the support of Labour MPs.
He was unable to withstand mounting calls to quit after Burnham had days earlier won a by-election that allowed him to return to parliament to launch a widely expected leadership challenge.
On the day Starmer announced his resignation, Burnham was sworn into parliament, becoming an MP again following his stint between 2001 and 2017.
Afterwards, some 200 Labour MPs feted Burnham during a group photo in Westminster, in a clear sign that they expect him to take over.
Former health minister Wes Streeting announced he was dropping his intention to run and throwing his support behind Burnham.
Burnham – seen as slightly to the left of the more centrist Starmer – is Labour's most popular politician, surveys show.
Many MPs feel he is the party's best chance of clawing back support from Nigel Farage's anti-immigrant Reform UK party before the next general election, expected in 2029.
Reform has led Labour in national opinion polls for well over a year, although the gap has narrowed in recent weeks.
Burnham has pledged fiscal discipline and to reduce the country's ballooning welfare bill, having already sought to calm markets by committing to the government's current borrowing limits.
And he has also proposed the creation of a "No. 10 North" to coordinate a devolution – a play on words on the UK prime minister's address at 10 Downing Street.
One Labour MP, who asked not to be named, said the party was right to "roll the dice" on Burnham, saying "he couldn't be worse than Starmer."
"I hope he's a breath of fresh air," she told AFP.
Another, also speaking on condition of anonymity, said he backed Burnham but was concerned that he will only have had a few weeks to prepare for government.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Affin refocuses SME BizChat on execution
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Affin Bank Bhd is shifting its annual Affin SME BizChat from thought leadership to practical execution.
This year's programme focuses on helping small and medium enterprises (SMEs) strengthen their financial resilience amid a challenging business environment.
Held at Menara Affin @ TRX today, the sixth edition of the programme centred on practical strategies to improve cash flow management, accelerate digital adoption, expand access to financing and strengthen ecosystem collaboration.
Themed "Financial Resilience for SMEs in Today's Economy", the event marks an evolution from previous editions, which explored topics such as women entrepreneurship, start-ups, international trade, digital transformation, artificial intelligence and environmental, social and governance (ESG) practices.
Affin Bank president and group chief executive officer Datuk Wan Razly Abdullah said the bank is committed to helping SMEs translate ideas into action as they navigate an increasingly dynamic operating environment.
"SMEs are central to Malaysia's economic resilience and future growth. In today's dynamic environment, businesses must be agile, disciplined and forward-looking in managing their finances.
"Through platforms like Affin SME BizChat, we are committed to empowering SMEs with the right insights, tools and ecosystem support to strengthen their resilience, embrace digitalisation and pursue sustainable growth opportunities," he said in a statement.
A panel discussion featuring representatives from Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation, TASConnect, CelcomDigi, Big Bad Wolf Books & BookXcess, Mampu AI and DHL Express shared practical insights on managing costs and cash flow.
The session also explored how SMEs can leverage automation and digital technologies to improve productivity, while tapping financing, strategic partnerships and regional expansion opportunities to diversify revenue streams.
The event also featured Affin Rakaniaga Bazaar, where entrepreneurs from the bank's SME Colony ecosystem showcased their products and services while engaging with potential customers and business partners.
Participants were introduced to AffinMAX, Affin's corporate internet banking platform that supports cash management, payments, trade services and day-to-day financial operations for businesses.
The programme concluded with The Elite Circle, an invitation-only networking session that connected selected businesses with Affin's strategic partners and key industry players to foster collaboration and business opportunities beyond the event.
Beyond the annual forum, the bank continues to support SMEs through a broad range of financial and non-financial solutions across different stages of business growth.
Affin is also providing access to Bank Negara Malaysia's RM5 billion SME Stabilisation Relief Facility (SME SRF), which offers eligible SMEs working capital financing of up to RM750,000 for up to five years at financing rates of up to 3.75 per cent per annum, inclusive of guarantee fees.
The facility is available until Dec 31, 2026, or until fully utilised.
The bank's continued focus on SMEs has supported growth in its Enterprise Banking business.
In the first quarter of 2026, gross loans and financing rose 12.6 per cent year-on-year, while the Enterprise Banking segment recorded a stronger 19.4 per cent increase.
AffinMAX had onboarded 63,542 customers as at December 2025, reflecting growing adoption of the bank's digital business banking platform.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 70-year-old woman loses RM183,100 in love scam after befriending a 'doctor' online
Article:
MUAR: A 70-year-old retired woman lost RM183,100 after falling victim to a love scam involving two impersonators she befriended through Facebook Messenger.
The victim alleged she befriended the suspects online.
One introduced himself as a doctor while his suspected accomplice claimed to be a corporate figure, with both alleging they worked overseas.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Raiz Mukhliz Azman Aziz said the victim began communicating with the two suspects in late April before they gained her trust and sought financial assistance.
He said the suspects claimed they needed money to settle legal fees related to a construction contract and to facilitate the release of a USD85,000 (RM340,000) cheque, which they said would be donated to a doctors' association.
"The victim was also promised a sum of money once the arrangements were completed," he said in a statement today.
The victim subsequently made eight money transfers into several bank accounts provided by the suspects, using her own savings, amounting to RM183,100.
However, after receiving the payments, the suspects continued demanding additional funds under various pretexts, leading the victim to realise she had been cheated.
She lodged a police report today.
The case is being investigated under Section 420 of the Penal Code for cheating, which carries a jail term of between one and 10 years, whipping and a fine upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aeon Credit Q1 profit jumps 23pct to RM95.16mil on loan growth
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Aeon Credit Service (M) Bhd recorded a higher net profit of RM95.16 million for the first financial quarter ended May 31, 2026 (Q1 FY27), up 22.7 per cent from RM77.55 million a year earlier, supported by stronger loan and financing growth.
Revenue for the quarter increased 7.9 per cent to RM647.57 million from RM599.92 million in the corresponding quarter last year, driven by higher financing activities.
The group said total transaction and financing volume rose 4.6 per cent year-on-year to RM2.35 billion during the quarter.
Gross financing receivables stood at RM16.07 billion as at May 31, 2026, increasing RM1.44 billion, or 9.8 per cent, from a year earlier.
After accounting for impairment losses, net financing receivables rose to RM15.25 billion from RM13.82 billion previously, representing a 10.4 per cent increase year-on-year.
However, asset quality saw a slight deterioration, with the non-performing loan (NPL) ratio edging up to 2.6 per cent from 2.57 per cent a year earlier.
Aeon Credit attributed the increase mainly to cost-of-living pressures affecting certain customer segments, particularly younger and lower-income groups.
The group said corrective measures have been implemented to address the rise in NPLs.
Its loan loss coverage ratio remained healthy at 195 per cent as at May 31, 2026, although lower than 217 per cent recorded a year earlier.
On prospects, the group said it adopts a cautious and prudent business approach, taking into consideration heightened geopolitical tensions in the Middle East which have disrupted global supply chains.
persistent inflationary pressures arising from elevated global oil prices and ongoing volatility in global financial markets.
The group remains focused on growing quality financing assets and continues to closely monitor inherent credit risks within its financing portfolio, while enhancing its information technology capabilities to improve operational efficiencies and maintain disciplined cost management.
Aeon Credit also said it will leverage the broader Aeon ecosystem in Malaysia, particularly through its 51 per cent subsidiary, AEON360 Sdn Bhd, to strengthen customer
loyalty, expand its customer base and enhance the overall customer experience.
Barring any unforeseen circumstances, the group expects to be able to sustain its business momentum through the continued implementation of appropriate strategic and operational measures for the financial year ending Feb 28, 2027.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Four, including two brothers, plead not guilty to running online gambling centre
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Four people, including two brothers, were charged in the magistrate's court here today with managing an online gambling operation in April.
Eow Yao Ping, 25, Eow Yong Huang, 30, Tan Yan Zhong, 25, and Cheng Chean Seng, 61, pleaded not guilty after the charge was read before magistrate Noor Farah Hazwani Osman.
They were accused of acting with common intention to manage a common gaming house used as an online gambling operations centre between 4.20pm and 6pm at Taman Kampung Raja here on April 7.
The charge was framed under Section 4(1)(c) of the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953, read together with Section 34 of the Penal Code.
If convicted, they face a fine of between RM5,000 and RM50,000, imprisonment of up to three years, or both.
Deputy public prosecutor Adlin Saffiya Kamaruzaman prosecuted, while the accused were represented by a lawyer from the National Legal Aid Foundation.
The prosecution proposed bail of RM8,000 for each accused with one surety, but the defence sought a lower amount, citing their clients' full cooperation with investigators and assurance that they would attend all court proceedings.
The court set bail at RM6,000 each with one surety and fixed Aug 11 for case mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hedge funds record best first-half performance in 13 years
Article:
LONDON: Global hedge funds delivered their strongest first-half performance since 2013, data from hedge fund data firm PivotalPath showed, as healthcare, tech and energy trades lifted returns in volatile markets.
In particular, April - when hedge funds returned 3.7 per cent in one month - proved to be the strongest April on record, the PivotalPath data showed.
Stock-trading hedge funds finished June with double-digit returns for the year so far, aided by their ability to successfully navigate already crowded trades, according to a Goldman Sachs client note that was seen by Reuters on Thursday.
Stockpickers returned 4.0 per cent last month, said the Goldman note from Wednesday. It noted that those hedge funds that use fundamental analysis to assess a company's financial health posted an 18.4 per cent return for the quarter, their strongest performance on Goldman's records. Their year-to-date result was slightly less at 17.4 per cent.
Bigger bets, wagers on healthcare, and joining trades that already had momentum proved successful, said Goldman.
Losses stemmed from how volatile markets turned in June, trading in a surging South Korean market and getting stuck in short bets that asset prices would fall, said the bank.
The second quarter was the best on record for the US SOX chip index, but June was the worst month for the Magnificent Seven.
The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF fell 9.0 per cent in June, its biggest monthly drop in over a year.
Oil prices have returned to pre-Iran war levels and markets expect at least one Federal Reserve rate hike by year-end, even as the latest US jobs number tempered traders' rate hike bets.
Hedge funds that use systematic models assessing market dynamics to pick trades were left with a 1.1 per cent gain in June after losses that came just at month's end. This group gained an 11.3 per cent return for the year to date, according to Goldman.
For systematic traders, losses came from volatile trading in the biggest US companies and Chinese firms, said a separate note from the US$18 billion hedge fund Winton, a systematic fund which tracks the performance of its competitors.
Short positioning in fixed income, particularly in long-dated US Treasuries, detracted from performance.
Hedge funds trading a range of different asset classes in global markets, like trend followers and commodity trading advisers, made money in the Canadian dollar and Japanese yen. But bigger losses in the Australian dollar, sterling and Norwegian krone eclipsed winnings, said Winton.
Many systematic strategies have limits on the amount of time they must remain in trades. Faster strategies were able to navigate choppier markets with more ease, said the Winton note.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Proton expands EV push with e.Mas 5 launch in Singapore
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Proton Holdings Bhd has expanded its electric vehicle (EV) presence in Singapore with the launch of the Proton e.Mas 5, marking the second model under its EV brand to enter the island republic after the Proton e.Mas 7.
The launch in Singapore forms part of the national carmaker's strategy to strengthen its regional footprint and grow its export business.
Proton International Corp chief executive officer Edmund Lim Meng Thong said the introduction of the Proton e.Mas 5 reflects growing confidence in Malaysian-made automotive products and reinforces the company's export ambitions.
"The launch of the Proton e.Mas 5 follows the encouraging reception for the Proton e.Mas 7 and reflects our long-term commitment to growing export volumes. It also strengthens Proton's position as a brand capable of delivering advanced technology, safety and value to customers across the region," he said in a statement.
Lim noted that Singapore, one of Southeast Asia's most competitive automotive markets, provides an important platform for brands seeking to establish a regional presence.
He said the expansion of the Proton e.Mas range in the country demonstrates Proton's ability to compete in mature EV markets with discerning consumers while supporting its broader international growth strategy.
"Proton's ambition goes beyond exporting vehicles. We aim to build a sustainable regional business through strong partnerships, excellent ownership experiences and products tailored to each market's mobility needs," he said.
The launch also highlights the growing role of the Proton e.Mas brand in Proton's business, supporting both Malaysia's transition to low-carbon mobility and the company's efforts to expand exports of Malaysian-developed mobility solutions.
The Proton e.Mas 5 is built on a platform that has achieved cumulative global sales of more than 610,000 units, together with its Geely equivalent, reflecting strong acceptance across multiple international markets.
In Malaysia, the Proton e.Mas 5 is currently the country's best-selling electric vehicle, reflecting the model's strong market appeal, while in  Singapore, customers purchasing the model will receive a range of ownership benefits like charging credits, S$5,000 worth of ownership, and cross-border warranty coverage.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China, Taiwan brace for Typhoon Bavi, possibly the most powerful storm in years
Article:
TOKYO: China and Taiwan were bracing for possibly the most destructive tropical storm in years as Typhoon Bavi churned southeast of Taiwan on Thursday, with winds near 200 kph, and as parts of China were still reeling from Typhoon Maysak.
Authorities in Taiwan urged residents to stock up on supplies and brace for what could be the most powerful typhoon to hit the island since 2024.
Bavi, currently about 1,000 km at its widest point or roughly the width of France, is forecast to skirt northern Taiwan before making landfall in China's eastern Fujian province on Saturday evening, according to China's National Meteorological Centre.
China is still reeling from Typhoon Maysak, which wreaked havoc across the southwestern region of Guangxi.
At least 39 people died due to the flooding caused by the storm in Guangxi, local officials told a news conference on Thursday. Nine people are still missing across the region, they added.
Bavi is set to be the largest storm by size to hit Taiwan since 1987, Jason Chang, Taiwan's Central Weather Administration forecaster, told Reuters, adding that storms of this size have been "fairly rare in recent years."
China, the world's second-largest economy, along with neighbouring Japan and Taiwan, are increasingly exposed to destructive weather events that scientists link to climate change.
This year is of particular concern because the expected emergence of El Nino could drive up temperatures and help fuel more frequent and intense typhoons.
If Bavi maintains its forecast intensity, it would be the most powerful typhoon, as hurricanes are known in the Asia-Pacific region, since Super Typhoon Kong-rey in 2024, according to AccuWeather, a commercial weather forecasting service.
"Some loss of wind intensity is anticipated starting Thursday, but Bavi will remain a dangerous storm as it impacts Taiwan and eastern China later Friday into Monday," according to Jason Nicholls, AccuWeather international forecasting expert.
In Taiwan's northeastern port town Suao, hundreds of fishing boats packed the harbour seeking shelter from the coming storm, as residents queued for sandbags from the local authorities and farmers rushed to harvest rice while the weather held.
[[nid:1484399]]
Chen Ming-hui, a 60-year-old captain of a 3-metric-tonne fishing vessel, said he hoped the typhoon would track further north and avoid a direct hit, recalling how previous storms had sunk boats and flooded the fishing town.
"Don't be fooled by the nice and calm weather now. A storm like this could be the most terrifying," Chen said, inspecting the ropes tightened on his boat.
Roughly 111 km southwest of Suao, in Japan's Okinawa prefecture, residents were warned by the country's meteorological agency to remain on high alert on Friday and Saturday for violent winds, landslides, flooding and storm surges.
The remnants of Typhoon Maysak spawned at least two inland tornadoes and major flooding in China's central Hubei province.
Two zebras, four porcupines, dozens of different types of parrots, a couple of North American raccoons, eight pheasants and various other animals - more than 100 in all - escaped from Guigang Zoo during the flooding, according to reports in Chinese state media.
Three lions at the zoo died in the floodwaters brought by Maysak, the Global Times reported.
One sika deer was rescued by local villagers, Wang Liyuan, the zoo's operator, was quoted as saying, while brown bears and wolves from the 'beast zone' were safe but in poor condition after nearly drowning.
Japan Airlines said it had cancelled 48 domestic flights scheduled for Friday because of the typhoon, affecting an estimated 7,610 passengers.
ANA said it would cancel 34 flights mainly serving Okinawa's Ishigaki and Miyako airports, affecting around 1,800 passengers on Friday, with 33 further domestic flights set to be cancelled on Saturday impacting 5,900 people.
"We should pay much attention to Bavi as it has spent a long time intensifying over the open Pacific, extracting energy from warm ocean and accumulating large amounts of moisture," said Xiangbo Feng, research scientist in tropical cyclones at Imperial College London.
"When it would make landfall or get close to coastal regions, the damage could be catastrophic. A small change in Bavi's track could have a significant influence," Feng added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor not Singapore's 'backyard', says PH's Tiram candidate
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor should not be considered Singapore's "backyard", says Pakatan Harapan's Tiram candidate Nor Zulaila Abdul Ghani.
She said Johor's strategic position, industrial strength and economic potential should not be overshadowed by its proximity to Singapore.
Nor Zulaila said Johor possessed its own strengths, including a skilled workforce, established industrial ecosystem, and strategic location, which placed the state in a position to become a regional economic powerhouse.
"The Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone development should further strengthen Johor's role as a strategic partner rather than a supporting player in the regional economy," she said.
[[nid:1484411]]
"Close cooperation with Singapore remains important and must continue to be strengthened, but the relationship must be based on mutual respect and mutual benefit," she said in a statement today.
Nor Zulaila said any suggestion that placed Johor in a subordinate position would undermine the aspirations and pride of Bangsa Johor.
She added that Johor had the potential to lead in high-value manufacturing, technology, innovation, logistics and the digital economy.
"Johor should not only support the growth of other economies, but must emerge as Malaysia's growth engine and a key gateway to Asean," she said.
Nor Zulaila said voters wanted leadership that was confident in Johor's capabilities and committed to building a state that was competitive, respected and economically independent.
"Johor is not anyone's backyard. Johor is Malaysia's future," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Johor housing model delivers more than 21,000 affordable homes, says Jafni
Article:
Jassmine Shadiqe
JOHOR BARU: Affordable housing has emerged as the centrepiece of BN Bukit Permai state seat incumbent's re-election campaign.
Caretaker state Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Md Jafni Md Shukor is highlighting Johor's delivery of more than 21,000 affordable homes and expanded assistance for first-time buyers as key achievements.
Jafni, who is defending his seat in the state polls, said housing remains one of the biggest concerns for families, particularly young couples struggling to own their first home amid rising living costs.
He said the state government's approach focused on building more affordable units, while mechanisms were in place to reduce financial barriers through subsidies, assistance schemes and a more accessible application system.
"The government must ensure that owning a home is not just a dream for the people, especially young families and first-time buyers," he told the New Straits Times yesterday.
The Bukit Permai seat is set for a four-cornered battle, with Jafni facing challengers from PH, PN and Bersama.
The seat, which PH won in 2018 before BN reclaimed it in 2022 with a 4,755-vote majority, is expected to favour BN as opposition votes are split among three contenders.
Jafni said voters had raised housing, cost of living and local development as key issues.
"While other candidates are seeking to convince residents through political promises, BN has a track record of delivering proven solutions and remains committed to doing more."
Jafni said he was confident voters would continue to support the administration based on its track record, particularly initiatives introduced under the leadership of caretaker Johor menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.
He pointed to the completion and handover of 21,412 affordable homes across Johor as evidence of the state government's commitment to expanding home ownership opportunities.
Among the initiatives highlighted was the delivery of free homes for eligible groups through targeted housing assistance programmes, including Rumah Prihatin Johor, aimed at helping families in need.
Jafni said the state had also introduced financial support measures, including RM7,000 assistance for first-home buyers, to ease the burden of purchasing a property.
He said the assistance complemented the state's wider housing strategy, which included subsidies to make homes more affordable and support lower- and middle-income households.
Another key improvement, he said, was the introduction of the E-Rumah online system, which streamlined applications and allowed applicants to access housing information more easily.
"The process has been simplified so that the people do not have to go through complicated procedures to apply for affordable housing," he said.
Jafni said the digital platform also improved transparency by allowing applicants to track their applications and obtain updates without relying on manual processes.
He added that housing delivery was part of the Johor government's broader effort to improve quality of life, particularly as the state experiences rapid economic growth and increasing demand for housing.
At the constituency level, Jafni said his focus remained on ensuring local residents benefited directly from state development programmes, including housing opportunities, infrastructure improvements and community facilities.
He said continued support for the state government would allow existing initiatives to be expanded, especially with more young families seeking affordable and comfortable homes.
"The people want results, not just promises. We have shown what can be achieved through consistent planning and implementation," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] MACC probes election candidate over alleged corruption in Johor polls
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission is looking into allegations of corruption against an election candidate during the Johor election campaign.
MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman declined to disclose further details, saying the commission wanted to remain impartial and avoid jeopardising the ongoing election process.
"I do not want to comment further because the election is ongoing. We do not want to jeopardise any party. We want to remain independent so that we are not seen as siding with anyone.
"If there is corruption during the election, regardless of the political party, candidate, voter or supporter involved, corruption is corruption. In this particular case, it involves a candidate," he told reporters here today.
Halim said MACC would investigate any election-related corruption without fear or favour, adding that the commission would not distinguish between political parties or individuals when enforcing the law.
Earlier, he said Johor MACC had activated five operations rooms in Johor Baru, Segamat, Kluang, Batu Pahat and Mersing in conjunction with the 16th Johor election to facilitate the public in channelling information on corruption and abuse of power throughout the campaign period.
As of today, the commission has received three reports, including one lodged by an Election Campaign enforcement team member at the Batu Pahat MACC branch concerning alleged bribery involving an election candidate.
All reports are currently being assessed and investigated under the relevant laws.
Halim reiterated that MACC would not compromise with anyone found to have committed election-related corruption, regardless of position, political affiliation or background.
He also reminded candidates, political parties, supporters and voters to comply with the law throughout the campaign and polling period, stressing that the integrity of the democratic process must be safeguarded.
"We want Malaysians to cast their votes with confidence that their voices cannot be bought, influenced or traded. MACC will continue to monitor the election and take firm action against anyone attempting to undermine democracy through corrupt practices," he said.
Halim said the commission would continue monitoring the Johor polls closely and urged members of the public to report any suspected acts of bribery or abuse of power, reiterating that every complaint would be investigated professionally, independently and based on evidence.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia and Thailand end seafood dispute with landmark agriculture MoU
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — Malaysia and Thailand have resolved outstanding issues related to fisheries market access and agreed to bring into force the Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on Agricultural Cooperation within one week.
The MoU was concluded here today in conjunction with Thailand’s Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul’s first official visit to Malaysia.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Anutin witnessed the exchange of the MoU between Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu and his Thai counterpart Suriya Juangroongruangkit.
Speaking at the joint press conference, Anwar said the agreement reflected both countries’ commitment to strengthening food security and expanding bilateral trade.
"We have resolved this issue of outstanding issues on fisheries with both ministers' commitment. They (Thailand) wanted the memorandum to come into effect in one and a half months, and we have agreed it should come into effect in one week,” he said.
Anutin also welcomed the effort, saying that food security was a key focus of the discussions.
"On food security, we welcome the meeting between our Agriculture Ministers to find amicable solutions in which we have concluded a moment ago to the pending cases of market access for fishery and agricultural products,” he said.
Meanwhile, in a statement, the Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security said the MoU provides a comprehensive framework for cooperation in strategic areas, including crop production, livestock, fisheries, trade facilitation and the marketing of agricultural products.
The agreement also covers research and development (R&D), capacity building, biosecurity, sanitary and phytosanitary (SPS) measures, and other areas of mutual interest.
The ministry said the framework would strengthen communication and coordination between the relevant authorities through a more structured, transparent and continuous mechanism, enabling agriculture and agri-food issues to be addressed more quickly, effectively and proactively.
"The two leaders had also held bilateral talks last week, focusing on expanding cooperation in trade, investment, agriculture, food security, and regional and international issues of mutual concern,” it said.
On June 1, Malaysia tightened import controls on Thai fisheries products by requiring Certificates of Analysis (CoA) for barramundi and imposing a temporary import restriction on five specific shrimp species.
The temporary restriction covers Penaeus esculentes (brown tiger prawn), Fenneropenaeus merguiensis (banana prawn), Penaeus vannamei (whiteleg shrimp), Penaeus monodon (giant tiger prawn) and Penaeus stylirostris (blue shrimp).
Agricultural and agri-food trade between Malaysia and Thailand continued to expand, reaching RM20.33 billion between January and December 2025.
Malaysia’s main exports to Thailand include coffee, cocoa, tea, spices and processed food products, while its principal imports comprise meat and meat-based products. — Bernama

Title: Johor election: How to find your right polling centre this weekend
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 9 — Registered voters for the 16th Johor state election have been advised to check their designated polling centres and voting streams ahead of polling day this Saturday.
The Election Commission (EC) said voters can do so through the
MySPR Semak portal
or the MySPR Semak mobile application.
“Let’s fulfil our responsibility as voters in the 16th Johor state election,” the EC said in a Facebook post today.
A total of 2.7 million eligible ordinary voters are expected to cast their ballots on July 11 to elect 56 state assembly representatives for the next term.
Last Tuesday, 19,471 early voters comprising 7,960 military personnel and their spouses, as well as 11,511 police personnel, including those from the General Operations Force (GOF) and their spouses, cast their ballots.
The 16th Johor state election is witnessing 172 candidates contesting 56 state seats, comprising 56 candidates each from Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN), 33 from Perikatan Nasional (PN), 15 from Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama), and four from the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (MUDA).
Also in the fray are candidates from the Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (ASLI) and the Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM), as well as six Independents. — Bernama

Title: MACC clears ex-minister Nik Nazmi of suspicious fund transfers in Dara, Amoi, Kelat elephant case
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has found no evidence of any suspicious fund transfers or financial transactions involving former Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad over allegations that he received RM53 million in bribes linked to the transfer of three elephants – Dara, Amoi and Kelat (DAK) – to Japan.
MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said preliminary checks through the Cash Threshold Report (CTR) and Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) confirmed that no suspicious financial transactions involving the former minister or companies linked to him had been detected.
“Based on investigations conducted so far, there is no evidence of any fund transfers or suspicious financial transactions involving the former minister or companies associated with him.
“However, investigations are continuing into four identified suspects, particularly in relation to financial and banking analyses,” he told a press conference here today.
